entities.name: Code Is Free
entities.context: Principle that AI-assisted code generation makes implementation cheap, but architecture/coordination/bugs remain expensive

entities.name: Fearless Refactoring
entities.context: Willingness to rewrite code from scratch when better abstractions emerge, enabled by cheap code generation

entities.name: Learning Code
entities.context: Exploratory code written to understand approaches, has infinite learning value but zero production value and should be deleted

entities.name: Sunk Cost Fallacy
entities.context: Anti-pattern where investment in one approach prevents switching to better solutions

entities.name: Prototypes
entities.context: Temporary implementations that must be deleted after serving their exploratory purpose

entities.name: Architecture
entities.context: Design planning that remains expensive and necessary even when code generation is free

entities.name: JSONL
entities.context: File format option for data persistence implementation

entities.name: SQLite
entities.context: Database option for data persistence implementation

entities.name: In-memory Dict
entities.context: Data structure option for lightweight persistence

entities.name: /tmp
entities.context: Temporary directory for storing learning and exploratory code

entities.name: Python
entities.context: Programming language used in learning code examples

entities.name: AI
entities.context: Enables rapid code generation making rewrites and exploration cheap

relationships.context: Cheap code generation enables willingness to rewrite without fear
Cheap generation makes exploratory coding viable then disposable
Cheap code means prototypes can be freely discarded
Learning code should live in /tmp to signal its temporary nature
Sunk cost thinking is the anti-pattern that fearless refactoring overcomes
Code is free but architecture planning remains expensive and necessary
AI code generation is what makes code cheap to produce
